LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY, UNITED HEALTH GROUP INC, UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH AND OPTUM, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS ENTERED INTO AN ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT COVERS THE DISPUTES AT ISSUE IN THE LAWSUIT. DEFENDANTS DEMANDED ARBITRATION AND PLAINTIFF REFUSED TO ARBITRATE. THE AGREEMENT IS VALID, ENFORCEABLE AND NOT UNCONSCIONABLE. SEE DOTSON V. AMGEN, INC. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 975; SERPA V. CALIFORNIA SURETY INVESTIGATIONS, INC. (2013) 215 CAL.APP.4TH 695; WILSON V. UNITED HEALTH GROUP, INC. 2012 WL 6088318 (E.D. CAL. 2012). THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE ON ACCOUNT OF LACK OF MUTUALITY OR ANY OTHER BASIS. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT HER CLAIMS TO ARBITRATION. THE PROCEEDINGS IN THIS COURT ARE STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION OR FURTHER ORDER OF THE COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-22-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BRIAN POULTER, ESQ., 213.413.6400, FOR PLAINTIFF BARBARA SMITH.
MICHAEL WAHLANDER, ESQ., 415.544.1015, FOR DEFENDANTS UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY, UNITED HEALTH GROUP INC. AND UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH AND OPTUN, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY, UNITED HEALTH GROUP INC, UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH and OPTUM, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration IS Granted. Defendants have shown that Plaintiff and Defendants entered into an arbitration agreement. The arbitration agreement covers the disputes at issue in the lawsuit. Defendants demanded arbitration and Plaintiff refused to arbitrate. The agreement is valid, enforceable and not unconscionable. See Dotson v. Amgen, Inc. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 975; Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695; Wilson v. United Health Group, Inc. 2012 WL 6088318 (E.D. Cal. 2012). The arbitration agreement is not substantively unconscionable on account of lack of mutuality or any other basis. Plaintiff is ordered to submit her claims to arbitration. The proceedings in this Court are stayed pending completion of arbitration or further order of the Court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
